meta.title: MAC Pocket Guide for Freshies 2026
meta.whatsappGroupSub: All admission updates, orientation, and help - one place. Tap Join.
meta.welcome: Welcome to MAC, fresher. This guide is written by seniors who've stood in the same queues, haggled with the same e-rickshaw bhaiya, and somehow figured it all out. Keep it bookmarked - you'll need it more than once.

union.intro: The Maharaja Agrasen College Student Union represents and supports the student body. Our primary goal is to ensure every student has a voice and that concerns reach the college administration. We also organise events and activities that enrich campus life - from orientation desks to Yuvaan, the college fest.
union.howWeHelp: Whether it's academic concerns, campus facilities, or extracurriculars, we are your first point of contact. Feel free to approach any elected member or reach us on Instagram / email.

fest.name: Yuvaan
fest.body: Yuvaan is MAC's annual college fest - the one week the whole campus turns into a stage. Last edition was organised by the ABVP-led MACSU. For the first time in MAC's history, two artists performed on the same fest: Karma and Shiva Chaudhary. Follow @yuvaan.mac for dates, passes, and line-ups.

admissionHelp.intro: Our Student Union Help Desk guides you through document verification, campus info, accommodation tips, course selection, and exam-form confusion. Seniors who've done this before - tap to call or DM on Instagram.

reach.intro: Maharaja Agrasen College, affiliated with Delhi University, is spread across a 10-acre campus in Vasundhara Enclave, East Delhi. It is conveniently located near three metro stations, ensuring easy accessibility from across the city and beyond.

metroStations.name: New Ashok Nagar
metroStations.note: Best pick if you're on RRTS / coming from further out

reach.erickshaw: From any of these stations, take an e-rickshaw - typically ₹20. We've all haggled with that bhaiya; don't pay more.

modes.title: Bus
modes.body: Nearby stops: Vasundhara Enclave and Noida Mod. Frequent DTC services to Delhi and Noida. For routes and schedules, use the DTC website or the One Delhi app.

modes.title: Auto / Cab
modes.body: The area is well-serviced by auto-rickshaws and taxis. Ola, Uber, and Rapido are widely available - useful for late evenings or when you're carrying bags on day one.

reach.closing: Whether you're using public transport or a ride-hailing app, getting to MAC is straightforward and affordable.

accommodation.intro: Three things that matter: where to take a PG/flat, what not to do, and what to pack.

where.title: Where to take a PG / flat
where.hostel: Girls: on-campus hostel exists but seats are limited - apply early. Meals, Wi-Fi, study areas, common rooms.
where.areasNote: These are closest / best connected to MAC by metro or e-rickshaw. Ask seniors in the WhatsApp group before you finalise.
lookFor: Close to metro or a short e-rickshaw ride to college
Near markets, pharmacies, and daily needs
Bed, wardrobe, Wi-Fi, study table (laundry/meals/cleaning are bonuses)
For flats: basic furnishings, stove/fridge if possible, reliable internet
Safety: CCTV, gated entry, guard - especially if you're new to Delhi
Shared rooms cut rent - live with people you can actually get along with
Always visit in person before paying any deposit
Proper rent agreement stating rent, deposit, and terms

donts.title: What not to do
items: Don't take basement rooms - damp, dark, poorly ventilated
Don't sign long lock-in periods - keep the option to leave if it's bad
Don't finalise food without a trial meal at the PG
Don't pay full deposit / advance without seeing the actual room
Don't skip checking washrooms, kitchen, and water supply
Don't ignore safety (unguarded buildings, no CCTV, shady brokers)
Don't accept verbal-only deals - get terms in writing
Don't move into cramped, poorly lit flats just because they're cheaper

packing.title: Packing guide
packing.intro: Bring these so day one isn't a shopping marathon.

items.items: Bedsheets, pillows, blankets; mattress topper helps
Basic utensils, kettle, snacks; portable induction if cooking is allowed
Toiletries, towels, detergent, cleaning supplies, first-aid kit
Study lamp, stationery; chair if the room doesn't have one
Power strip, fan; small fridge if not provided

electives.title: Skill Enhancement Course
electives.body: Focuses on technical and practical skills. Across 6 semesters you'll choose different SECs - each built to make you industry-ready in a practical domain.

electives.title: Value Addition Course
electives.body: Offered in the first 4 semesters. Builds your moral and ethical foundation - holistic development that shows up in personal and professional life.

electives.title: Generic Elective
electives.body: Mainly for honours students - a subject outside your major. Once chosen, it stays consistent and becomes your minor. For BA Prog students: Hindi or English as GE.

electives.title: Ability Enhancement Course
electives.body: EVS in one year, an Indian language in another. The sequence repeats across years for a well-rounded education.

academics.electivesNote: You'll receive the list of available SEC/VAC courses by email from the college. Choose what aligns with your interests and goals. More detail is in the prospectus on the college website and at orientation.

baProg.title: Special note for BA Programme
baProg.body: As a BAP student you choose a combination of two subjects. Each year, one is your major and the other your minor. You can change major and minor in the 3rd semester based on interest or academic goals.
how: Major = you study both the major and minor papers of that subject (more depth)
Minor = only the minor paper of that subject (broader secondary area)

example.title: Example: History + Political Science
steps: Year 1 - History major: major + minor papers in History, only the minor paper in Political Science
Year 2 - you may switch: Political Science becomes major (major + minor papers), History becomes minor paper only
If you keep History as major throughout and meet the minimum credits, History is your degree specialisation with Political Science as minor - reflected on your final degree

fees.note: * University share as decided by University of Delhi. Fees may be revised - confirm at the fee counter / college website.

conduct.title: Academic Integrity
conduct.body: Uphold honesty in assignments, exams, and projects. Plagiarism, cheating, or any academic dishonesty is strictly prohibited. Submit your own work; cite others properly.

conduct.title: Respect for Others
conduct.body: Respect everyone regardless of background, religion, gender, sexual orientation, or ethnicity. No discrimination, harassment, or bullying. Engage in civil dialogue - abusive language and intimidation are unacceptable. Attend classes regularly, be punctual, don't disrupt lectures.

conduct.title: Campus Behaviour
conduct.body: Keep the campus clean - use bins. Respect college property (furniture, equipment, facilities). Vandalism leads to disciplinary action.

conduct.title: Hostel Conduct
conduct.body: Follow hostel curfew. Late entries need prior permission. Visitors only as per hostel rules - unauthorised visitors not allowed.

conduct.title: Safety & Security
conduct.body: Always carry your college ID and present it when asked. Drugs, alcohol, and other intoxicants are strictly prohibited on campus and at college-hosted events.

conduct.title: Disciplinary Process & Appeal
conduct.body: Violations may mean warning, suspension, or expulsion depending on severity. You have the right to appeal by submitting a formal request to the disciplinary committee within the specified period.

conduct.title: Grievance Reporting
conduct.body: Report grievances on academics, hostel life, or interpersonal conflicts to the concerned authorities. Don't sit on problems.

conduct.title: Legal Compliance
conduct.body: Comply with all applicable laws including those of the University of Delhi. Illegal activities on or off campus have serious consequences.

committees.source: Official MAC website notices (Grievance Redressal reconstituted 13 Mar 2026; Admission Grievance page; Anti-Ragging office order 24 Jun 2025)

items.name: Anti-Ragging Committee
items.purpose: Curb ragging and keep the campus ragging-free (office order 24 Jun 2025).

items.name: Internal Complaints Committee (ICC)
items.extra: See the latest ICC notice on the college website - composition is updated periodically.

committees.fallback: Maintenance register at Counter No. 1. Unresolved issues: mac_su@mac.du.ac.in or gcr_mac@mac.du.ac.in. Full list: mac.du.ac.in/college-comittee.aspx

firstWeek: Join the Student Union WhatsApp group first - all admission and orientation updates land there
Carry originals + photocopies of admission documents and a few passport photos
Find the Student Union Help Desk on day 1 - they'll point you everywhere
Get your college ID card process started early
Locate canteen, photocopy shop, and stationery on campus
Save this guide offline (Add to Home Screen) before you lose signal in the metro
Attend orientation - electives and BA Prog structure get explained properly there
Follow @macsu_official and @yuvaan.mac so you don't miss fest and society updates

faq.q: What's the e-rickshaw fare from metro?
faq.a: Around ₹20 from New Ashok Nagar / Noida Sec-15 / Trilokpuri. Push back if quoted higher.

faq.q: Where do I get my ID card?
faq.a: Through the college admin process after admission - ask the Student Union Help Desk on day 1.
DU has minimum attendance requirements for exams. Details come at orientation - take them seriously.

faq.q: When do I choose SEC / VAC / GE?
faq.a: Lists come by college email. Orientation covers this. Don't choose based on FOMO alone.
On-campus hostel is for girls only (limited). Boys look at PGs/flats nearby - see Accommodation.

faq.q: Who do I call for admission confusion?
faq.a: Student Union Help Desk numbers on this guide, or the official college admission helpdesk under Help & Safety.

faq.q: How do I join a society?
faq.a: Watch for auditions/orientations in the first weeks. Follow society Instagrams and @macsu_official.
MAC's annual fest - dates land on @yuvaan.mac. Last edition was by the ABVP-led MACSU, with Karma and Shiva Chaudhary performing (first time two artists in MAC fest history).

faq.q: Fees look different from what I paid?
faq.a: Confirm at the fee counter. University share can change; figures here are from the official 2026-27 structure.

credits.built: Made with care by your seniors - Student Union 2025-26 × ABVP
credits.note: Successor to the Pocket Guide For Freshies 2024. Update names and numbers in data/guide.json each year.
